overview: The deck moves from the failure mode of naive long-form generation to a structured, multi-stage agent design for screenplays, closing with how that pipeline generalizes.

acts.name: Problem: long-form generation limits
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: Why one-shot writing drifts and loses coherence.

acts.name: Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: Premise, arc, and scene-level agents for structured output.

acts.name: Synthesis: end-to-end flow and takeaways
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: How the pieces connect and generalize beyond screenplays.

transitions: From “Problem: long-form generation limits” to “Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline”: the deck moves from Why one-shot writing drifts and loses coherence.… to Premise, arc, and scene-level agents for structured output.…

From “Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline” to “Synthesis: end-to-end flow and takeaways”: the deck moves from Premise, arc, and scene-level agents for structured output.… to How the pieces connect and generalize beyond screenplays.…
ending_function: Students should see long-form generation as a planning problem solvable with staged agents.
